content: Hey, I'm so excited to finally have some free time to work on my YouTube channel. I've been planning it for months.
That sounds like a great project! What kind of content are you planning to create for your channel?
I'm thinking of sharing my passion for salsa dancing and maybe some philosophy discussions. I've been reading a lot of Nietzsche lately.
Nietzsche is a great choice. I've heard his ideas can be quite thought-provoking. Have you considered collaborating with other dancers or philosophers for your channel?
Actually, I've been talking to my colleague, Dr. Lee, from City Hospital where I work as an ER nurse. She's really into philosophy too and might be interested in participating.
That's awesome! It's always great to have someone to bounce ideas off of. What made you decide to start a YouTube channel now?
I turned 57 last month and realized I want to pursue my hobbies more seriously. Plus, I've been playing piano for years and thought it would be nice to share that with a wider audience.
Happy belated birthday! It's never too late to start a new project. Do you have any specific goals for your channel, like a certain number of subscribers or views?
Well, I'd like to reach at least 1,000 subscribers within the next 6 months. And maybe even get to perform at a salsa festival in Miami next year.
Those are great goals. Miami sounds like a fantastic destination for a salsa festival. Have you traveled there before?
Yes, I went there with my family for New Year's Eve in 2018. We had a blast. My daughter, Sophia, loved the beach.
That sounds like a wonderful trip. I'm sure your daughter had a great time. Does she share your interest in salsa dancing or piano?
Actually, Sophia is more into sports, but my wife, Rachel, is really supportive of my hobbies and has even taken a few salsa lessons with me.
It's great to have a supportive family. I wish you the best of luck with your YouTube channel and your salsa dancing endeavors.
